title: nasturtium aquaticum
intro: Nasturtium aquaticum, also known as watercress, is a floating aquatic plant that grows in shallow water and is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like calcium and iron, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Watercress is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, salads, and juices, to take advantage of its medicinal and nutritional properties.
